metadata.title: Differences in Circulating Gluconeogenic Substrates During Short-Term Fasting in Men, Women, and Children
metadata.study_design: Comparative fasting study with three groups: 10 adult men fasted for 86 hr, 10 adult women fasted for 86 hr, and 15 prepubertal children fasted for 30 hr

evidence.quote: Differences in Circulating Gluconeogenic Substrates During Short-Term Fasting in Men, Women, and Children
Morey W. Haymond, Irene E. Karl, William L. Clarke, Anthony S. Pagliara, and Julio V. Santiago
20 adults (10 men and 10 women) were fasted for 86 hr and 15 children (6.1 _+ 0.8 yr, mean + SE) were fasted for 30 hr

methods_participants.label: Fasting Duration - Adults
methods_participants.value: at least 200 gm carbohydrate per day for three days prior to study

evidence.quote: 20 adults (10 men and 10 women) were fasted for 86 hr

methods_participants.label: Assay Method - Glucose, Alanine, Glutamine, Lactate, Pyruvate, Beta-hydroxybutyrate, Acetoacetate

evidence.quote: Plasma glucose, alanine, glutamine and glutamate and blood lactate, pyruvate, beta-bydroxybutyrate and acetoacetate were determined microfluorometrically as previously described.

methods_participants.label: Amino Acid Analysis Method
methods_participants.value: Spinco-Beckman 119 Amino Acid Analyzer using standard physiologic methodology

evidence.quote: Amino acid determinations were performed on Spinco-Beckman 119 Amino Acid Analyzer using standard physiologic methodology.
cortisol by a protein binding assay

methods_participants.label: Amino Acid Sampling - Children
methods_participants.value: Between 1800-2000 hr, 0800 hr, and subsequently at 4 hr intervals

evidence.quote: Bloods were obtained between 1800-2000 hr, 0800 hr, and subsequently at 4 hr intervals.

methods_participants.label: Blood sampling - Initial timing
methods_participants.value: Following an evening meal (1700-1800 hr), bloods obtained between 1800-2000 hr

evidence.quote: All individuals were fasted following an evening meal (1700 1800 hr). Bloods were obtained between 1800-2000 hr
all had fasting glucose concentrations < 110 mg/dl

methods_participants.label: Inclusion criteria - Adult carbohydrate intake
methods_participants.value: at least 200 gm carbohydrate per day for three days prior to the study

evidence.quote: The adults were consuming at least 200 gm carbohydrate per day for three days prior to the study

methods_participants.label: Blood Collection - Volume and Anticoagulant
methods_participants.value: 4 to 5 milliliter of blood with Na heparin (0.1 ml)

evidence.quote: Four to five milliliter of blood were drawn into a syringe containing Na heparin (0.1 ml)

methods_participants.label: Amino acid sampling frequency - Men and women
methods_participants.value: Determined on the 0, 14, 22, and 30 hr fasted samples of five children

evidence.quote: on the 0, 14, 22, and 30 hr fasted samples of five children

methods_participants.label: Inclusion criteria - No diabetes family history
methods_participants.value: None of the subjects had a history compatible with hypoglycemic episodes or documented hypoglycemia

evidence.quote: None of the subjects had a history compatible with hypoglycemic episodes or documented hypoglycemia

narrative_results.statement: Glucose concentrations were similar in the three groups studied during the first 18 hr of fasting

evidence.quote: Glucose concentrations were similar in the three groups studied during the first 18 hr of fasting (Fig. 1).

narrative_results.statement: An inverse relationship was observed between plasma glucose and beta-hydroxybutyrate concentrations in each group

evidence.quote: An inverse relationship (p < 0.001) was observed between plasma glucose and beta-hydroxybutyrate concentrations in each group (r = 0.934, 0.925, 0.961, men, women and children, respectively).

discrepancies.pass_2_value: Pass 1 describes Insulin Assay Method (radioimmunoassay) while Pass 2 describes Cortisol Assay Method (protein binding assay). These are different analytes with different assay methods.
Pass 1 describes Growth Hormone Assay Method (radioimmunoassay) while Pass 2 describes Inclusion Criteria for Fasting Glucose. These are completely different facts.
Pass 1 describes Inclusion Criteria for Fasting Glucose while Pass 2 describes Inclusion Criteria for Family History. These are different inclusion criteria.
Pass 1 describes Family History inclusion criteria while Pass 2 describes Hypoglycemia History inclusion criteria. These are different criteria.
Pass 1 states 10 men only; Pass 2 states 10 men and 10 women. Incompatible population descriptions

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Beta-hydroxybutyrate and acetoacetate concentrations rose more rapidly and to higher values in the children (3.75 _+ 0.35 mM and 1.26 _+ 0.12 mM, respectively) than in the adults (0.91 _ 0.15 mM and 0.38 +_ 0.06 mM, respectively in the men and 1.68 _+ 0.20 mM and 0.62 _+ 0.06 mM in the women) over the initial 30 hr fasting (p < 0.001).
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Beta-hydroxybutyrate and acetoacetate concentrations rose more rapidly and to higher values in the children (3.75 _+ 0.35 mM and 1.26 _+ 0.12 mM, respectively) than in the adults (0.91 _ 0.15 mM and 0.38 +_ 0.06 mM, respectively in the men and 1.68 _+ 0.20 mM and 0.62 _+ 0.06 mM in the women)

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Between 6 and 30 hr of fasting, blood lactate concentrations were higher in children than in adults
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Blood lactate concentrations were higher in children than in adults between 6 and 30 hr of fasting

semantic_groups.rationale: Pass 1 describes study population (10 women), Pass 2 describes fasting duration (86 hours). These are different facts with no overlap.
Both describe 15 children in study population. Pass 2 adds age detail (6.1 ± 0.8 yr) which is complementary, not contradictory.
Pass 1 describes children's age (6.1 ± 0.8 years), Pass 2 describes fasting duration for children (30 hours). These are completely different facts.
Pass 1 describes fasting duration for adults (86 hours), Pass 2 describes amino acid sampling methodology for men and women. These are unrelated facts.
Pass 1 describes fasting duration for children (30 hours), Pass 2 describes amino acid sampling methodology for children. These are different facts.
Both describe glucose assay method as microfluorometric/microfluorometrically. Minor wording variation (adverb vs adjective form) with same meaning.
Both identify Spinco-Beckman 119 Amino Acid Analyzer as the method. Pass 2 adds methodological context but describes the same instrument.
Both describe glucagon assay method as radioimmunoassay. Identical core fact with identical wording.
Pass 1 describes Insulin Assay Method (radioimmunoassay) while Pass 2 describes Cortisol Assay Method (protein binding assay). These are different analytes with different assay methods.
Pass 1 describes Growth Hormone Assay Method (radioimmunoassay) while Pass 2 describes Inclusion Criteria for Fasting Glucose. These are completely different facts.
Pass 1 contains Cortisol Assay Method information but Pass 2 is empty. This is an orphan entry.
Both describe the same blood sampling timing with negligible wording differences (addition of 'Between' in Pass 2).
Pass 1 contains Fasting Start Time information but Pass 2 is empty. This is an orphan entry.
Pass 1 describes Inclusion Criteria for Fasting Glucose while Pass 2 describes Inclusion Criteria for Family History. These are different inclusion criteria.
Both describe the same carbohydrate intake requirement with identical values. Minor difference is 'gm' vs 'grams' which is negligible.
Pass 1 describes Family History inclusion criteria while Pass 2 describes Hypoglycemia History inclusion criteria. These are different criteria.
Pass 1 contains information about hypoglycemia history inclusion criteria; Pass 2 is empty
Both describe blood collection volume of 4-5 ml. Pass 2 adds anticoagulant detail (Na heparin) not in Pass 1, but core volume information is consistent
Pass 1 contains amino acid sampling information for men and women; Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 contains amino acid sampling information for children; Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 states 10 men only; Pass 2 states 10 men and 10 women. Incompatible population descriptions
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
Same fact expressed with different word order; temporal clause repositioned but meaning unchanged
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
